summary_oneline: ScrapingFlow: composable long-horizon scraping via TemplatePlan, ScrapingFlow DAG, and FlowExecutor built atop execute_plan_steps

localization.role: Step runner invoked per-node by FlowExecutor; accepts pre-initialized driver, returns ScrapingResult
Immutable plan model produced by TemplatePlan.bind(); fingerprint is URL-only
16-char SHA-256 fingerprint; template fingerprints must incorporate param values
29 registered action types; Loop uses single-brace {index} convention
Loop action with template vars; uses single braces, not double braces as brainstorm claims
Generic registry base class; 3-tier lookup; extension pattern for TemplatePlanRegistry
Demonstrates BasePlanRegistry extension pattern: separate index, failure tracking, file storage
Translation pattern to follow for TemplatePlan.bind()
Semaphore-based concurrency pattern reusable for fan-out; class itself too URL-centric
Single-context driver; FlowExecutor needs direct Playwright access for multi-context
19 abstract methods; no context/page management; PageDriver adapter must implement all
29 public exports; new types must be added here

constraints.statement: ScrapingPlan is immutable — no mutation methods, fingerprint auto-computed from URL in model_post_init
constraints.implication: TemplatePlan.bind() must produce a NEW ScrapingPlan, not mutate one; fingerprint must incorporate param values to avoid collisions

constraints.statement: execute_plan_steps expects a pre-initialized AbstractDriver and does not manage driver lifecycle
constraints.implication: FlowExecutor must create and manage Page-wrapping drivers itself, passing them to execute_plan_steps per node

constraints.statement: Loop, conditional, authenticate, and 5 other advanced actions are SKIPPED in standalone executor
constraints.implication: Flow nodes containing Loop/conditional steps won't execute correctly via execute_plan_steps alone — need WebScrapingTool or custom dispatch

constraints.statement: PlaywrightDriver creates only ONE BrowserContext; AbstractDriver exposes no context/page management
constraints.implication: FlowExecutor must bypass PlaywrightDriver and work directly with Playwright's Browser object for multi-session flows

constraints.statement: CrawlEngine's concurrency is asyncio.Semaphore+gather but its interface is URL-centric (scrape_fn(url, plan))
constraints.implication: Reuse the semaphore pattern, not the CrawlEngine class, for flow fan-out

constraints.statement: No checkpoint mechanism exists anywhere in the scraping module
constraints.implication: Flow execution checkpoints must be built from scratch — suggest file-based JSON per execution run

constraints.statement: Loop template substitution uses single braces {index}, not double {{index}} as brainstorm states
constraints.implication: TemplatePlan should use a DIFFERENT convention (double braces {{param}} recommended) to avoid collision with Loop's single-brace vars and CSS/JSON syntax

constraints.statement: BasePlanRegistry[T] is generic with ExtractionPlanRegistry as proven extension pattern
constraints.implication: TemplatePlanRegistry can follow the same pattern: separate index file, per-fingerprint storage

new.name: TemplatePlan
new.role: Pydantic model with ParamSpec list + url_template/objective_template/steps_template; bind(**kwargs) validates params and produces a concrete ScrapingPlan

new.name: ParamSpec
new.role: Pydantic model defining a typed parameter: name, type (string|int|date|enum|url), required, default, choices, description

new.name: ScrapingFlow
new.role: Pydantic model: DAG of FlowNodes with data-dependency inputs and session affinity labels; validates refs/cycles on load

new.name: FlowNode
new.role: Pydantic model: id, plan_ref, inputs dict, session label, on_error policy

new.name: FlowExecutor
new.role: Orchestration engine: topological sort, session lifecycle (lazy open BrowserContext per session label, deterministic close by last-use), per-node execution via execute_plan_steps, fan-out concurrency, checkpoint persistence

new.name: FlowResult
new.role: Aggregated result container: per-node ExtractionResult/ScrapingResult, flow-level success flag, timing, checkpoint state

new.name: PageDriver
new.role: Lightweight AbstractDriver adapter wrapping a Playwright Page object; delegates all 19 abstract methods to Page equivalents

new.name: SessionManager
new.role: Owns the Playwright Browser instance; creates/caches/closes BrowserContexts by session label; deterministic lifecycle based on precomputed last-use

modified.change: Add exports: TemplatePlan, ParamSpec, ScrapingFlow, FlowNode, FlowExecutor, FlowResult

untouched: ScrapingPlan — no modification; TemplatePlan.bind() produces it
execute_plan_steps — used as-is; called per node
AbstractDriver — interface unchanged; PageDriver implements it
PlaywrightDriver — unchanged; FlowExecutor works at a lower level (Playwright Browser)

patterns_to_follow.pattern: asyncio.Semaphore + gather for bounded concurrency (from CrawlEngine._run_concurrent)
Pydantic BaseModel for all data models (consistent with ScrapingPlan, ExtractionPlan)

integration_risks.risk: PageDriver adapter must implement all 19 AbstractDriver abstract methods
integration_risks.impact: High implementation surface; some methods (start/quit) need special handling since PageDriver doesn't own the browser lifecycle
integration_risks.mitigation: Map each method to Playwright Page equivalent; start() is no-op; quit() closes the page only

integration_risks.risk: Loop/conditional actions silently skipped in standalone executor
integration_risks.impact: Flow nodes with Loop steps produce incomplete results without error
integration_risks.mitigation: Either route nodes with advanced actions through WebScrapingTool instead of execute_plan_steps, or port the _exec_loop logic to a reusable function callable from FlowExecutor

integration_risks.risk: Concurrent fan-out on shared authenticated session (multiple Pages in one BrowserContext)
integration_risks.impact: Cookie writes from parallel requests may race
integration_risks.mitigation: Defer to sequential execution within a session; document as known limitation; add concurrency support per-session in a future iteration

confidence_map.statement: execute_plan_steps can be invoked per-node with any AbstractDriver-conformant adapter
confidence_map.reasoning: Function signature directly confirmed; accepts any AbstractDriver, no lifecycle coupling

confidence_map.statement: ScrapingPlan fingerprint is URL-only; template fingerprints must incorporate param values
confidence_map.reasoning: _compute_fingerprint reads normalized_url only; two binds with different params on same URL template would collide

confidence_map.statement: Loop template substitution uses single braces {index}, contradicting brainstorm's claim of double braces
confidence_map.reasoning: Regex pattern r'\{([^}]*(?:i|index|iteration)[^}]*)\}' confirmed in tool.py:3271

confidence_map.statement: CrawlEngine's Semaphore concurrency pattern is reusable for fan-out
confidence_map.reasoning: Standard asyncio.Semaphore + gather pattern; straightforward to replicate

confidence_map.statement: BasePlanRegistry is generic and supports new plan types via subclassing
confidence_map.reasoning: ExtractionPlanRegistry already proves the extension pattern

confidence_map.statement: PlaywrightDriver cannot manage multiple BrowserContexts — FlowExecutor must bypass it
confidence_map.reasoning: start() creates exactly one context; no API to create more

confidence_map.statement: A lightweight PageDriver adapter wrapping Playwright Page is feasible for execute_plan_steps
confidence_map.reasoning: Most AbstractDriver methods map to Playwright Page equivalents, but 19 methods need implementation; start/quit semantics differ

confidence_map.statement: Flow checkpoint persistence must be built from scratch — no existing mechanism
confidence_map.reasoning: CrawlEngine has no checkpoints; registry persistence is index-level, not execution-level

confidence_map.statement: Loop/conditional actions will be silently skipped in flow nodes using execute_plan_steps
confidence_map.reasoning: Confirmed: executor logs warning and returns True for loop/conditional action types

confidence_map.statement: ExtractionPlan.to_scraping_plan() is the correct pattern for TemplatePlan.bind()
confidence_map.reasoning: Both transform a rich model into an executable ScrapingPlan; bind() adds parameter rendering

confidence_map.statement: No existing template/flow/parameterized patterns in the scraping module
confidence_map.reasoning: grep returned no matches; all three capabilities are net-new

unknowns.question: What is the exact mini-grammar for the inputs resolver ('node_id.path.field')? Should it support list indexing (e.g., 'listing.products[0].url'), nested dict access, or just flat field lookup on ExtractedEntity.fields?
plausible_answers: JSONPath-lite: support nested access via dot notation and array indexing

unknowns.question: Should flow nodes with Loop/conditional steps route through WebScrapingTool (full context, heavier) or should the Loop dispatch logic be extracted and reused in FlowExecutor?
plausible_answers: Route through WebScrapingTool — reuse existing implementation, accept heavier weight
Extract loop dispatch into a reusable function — cleaner but requires refactoring tool.py
Defer — document as limitation; loops in flow nodes are a future enhancement

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence across all affected modules, the scope is well-defined with 3 clear new capabilities, and the brainstorm already explored and rejected alternatives. The two unknowns are implementation-level decisions resolvable during spec writing.

meta.notes: The brainstorm document's claim about double-brace convention {{index}} in Loop is factually incorrect — Loop uses single braces. This is the most significant discrepancy between the brainstorm and actual codebase. The PlaywrightDriver's single-context limitation is the biggest architectural gap to bridge.
